pages.title: llms.txt
pages.content:
> Notion is the connected workspace where better, faster work happens. From notes and docs to projects and wikis, Notion's highly customizable building blocks make it the all-in-one workspace for modern teams. More than 50 million people and teams around the world use Notion to organize their work and get more done.

Key terms: Workspace, Knowledge Management, Project Management, Wiki, Documents, Database, Collaboration, Team Productivity, AI Writing Assistant, API, Templates, Blocks, Wikis, Notes, Tasks, Knowledge Base

## Core Products

- [AI](https://www.notion.com/product/ai): AI-powered writing, analysis, and productivity assistance built right into your workspace.
- [Enterprise Search](https://www.notion.com/product/notion-ai-for-work): Get the right information, wherever it lives
- [Calendar](https://www.notion.com/product/calendar): Connected calendar that brings your tasks, meetings, and deadlines into one view.
- [Notes & Docs](https://www.notion.com/product/docs): Powerful and beautiful docs with AI assistance, real-time collaboration, and rich media support.
- [Mail](https://www.notion.com/product/mail): The inbox that organizes itself, drafts emails, and schedules meetings any way you'd like.
- [Projects](https://www.notion.com/product/projects): Flexible project management with customizable views, automation, and team collaboration.
- [Sites](https://www.notion.com/product/sites): The easiest way to get a website up and running.
- [Wikis](https://www.notion.com/product/wikis): Create a comprehensive knowledge base that grows with your team.
- [Forms](https://www.notion.com/product/forms): Create and share unlimited forms for free, right in Notion.
- [Charts](https://www.notion.com/product/charts): Monitor progress, spot trends, and take action using your Notion data.
- [Automations](https://www.notion.com/md/automations): Work smarter with automations. Stay ahead and up-to-date without all the manual effort.

## Solutions By Team Types

- [Startups](https://www.notion.com/startups): Build faster with Notion's AI-powered apps. Get up to 6 months free with Notion AI – over $6,000 in savings.
- [Small teams](https://www.notion.com/teams): Your projects, wikis and docs, all side by side. For small teams to large organizations.
- [Enterprise](https://www.notion.com/enterprise): The tool teams love is easy to implement, and checks all your admin, security & compliance needs.
- [Non-Profits](https://www.notion.com/nonprofits): We support 501(c)3 organizations working to solve the world's toughest problems with 50% off our Plus Plan.

## Solutions By Role

- [Managers](https://www.notion.com/product/notion-for-managers): Connect the dots for your team. It's one tool where work comes together – planning, people, meeting notes, projects, and more.
- [Engineering](https://www.notion.com/product/notion-for-engineering): Build custom workflows to manage sprints alongside documentation. Minimize work about work and create as a team.
- [Education/Students](https://www.notion.com/product/notion-for-education): One place to manage all your schoolwork, clubs, and side projects. In the classroom and beyond.
- [Product](https://www.notion.com/product/notion-for-product): Bring together all the people and info you need to get new products into the world fast.
- [Design](https://www.notion.com/product/notion-for-design): House all your design assets, specs, and projects, and connect them in Notion. So your team can focus on designing.
- [Teams](https://www.notion.com/product/notion-for-teams): Tool overload kills team productivity. Get more done by combining all your tools in one with Notion.

## Getting Started
- [Request a Demo](https://www.notion.com/contact-sales): Get help with pricing and plans, schedule a demo, explore use-cases for your team, and more.
- [Guides](https://www.notion.com/help/guides): Sharpen your skills and explore new ways to use Notion.
- [Consulting Partners](https://www.notion.so/consulting-partners): Notion Solutions Partners are trusted productivity experts and solutions architects.
- [Help Center](https://www.notion.com/help): For every question you'll have about Notion; All in one spot.
- [Notion Academy](https://www.notion.com/help/notion-academy): Get started with Notion for your role or use-case. Follow curated learning pathways to build custom solutions to every day problems.
- [Webinars](https://www.notion.com/webinars): Live sessions every week covering all the Notion basics. Want to join? Here's when they're happening next.
- [Integrations](https://www.notion.com/integrations): Stop switching between apps to get work done. Keep info flowing in and out of Notion and reduce overhead with our integrations.
- [Developers](https://developers.notion.com/): Connect Notion pages and databases to the tools you use every day, creating powerful workflows.
- [Community](https://www.notion.com/community): Join the global community of Notion users and creators.
- [Template Gallery](https://www.notion.com/templates): Browse hundreds of pre-built templates for any use case.

## Popular Resources

- [Notion Blog](https://www.notion.com/blog): Thoughts on the future of work, from the people and teams creating it.
- [Customer Stories](https://www.notion.com/customers): Explore real workflows that save companies time, eliminate old tools, and bring peace of mind.
- [Help Center](https://www.notion.com/help): Comprehensive guides and documentation
- [Developer Docs](https://developers.notion.com): API documentation and developer resources
- [Security](https://www.notion.com/security): Learn about Notion's security practices
- [Pricing](https://www.notion.com/pricing): Simple, transparent pricing for teams of all sizes
- [Notion Faces](https://www.notion.com/faces): Bring your best ideas, boldest resolutions, and now a custom self-portrait to life in Notion, the workspace shaped by you.
- [Releases](https://www.notion.com/releases): Follow along with every new release.

## Downloads
- [Mobile](https://www.notion.com/mobile): Work on the go with the Notion app.
- [Desktop](https://www.notion.com/desktop): Download the Notion desktop app for a faster experience
- [Calendar](https://www.notion.com/product/calendar/download): Notion Calendar can do more on your desktop.
- [Web Clipper](https://www.notion.com/web-clipper): Save any page on the web to Notion. We'll help you turn them into action.

## Contact & Support

- [Help & Support](https://www.notion.com/help): Get help from our support team
- [Contact Sales](https://www.notion.com/contact-sales): Talk to our sales team about enterprise plans
- [Status](https://www.notion-status.com): Check Notion's system status
- [Twitter](https://twitter.com/NotionHQ): Follow Notion for updates
- [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/notionhq): Connect with Notion on LinkedIn
- [Careers](https://www.notion.com/careers): Open roles with Notion

Get started

# Overview

Copy page

Discover what Notion connections are, when to use each type, and what you can build.

Copy page

## 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#using-notion-api)

Using Notion API

Notion connections let you connect your workspace to external tools and automate workflows through code. With the REST API, you can read, create, and update nearly everything in a workspace — pages, databases, users, comments, and more. When you create a connection, you define what it can do: which API endpoints it can call, what content it can read or write, and how it authenticates. Each connection gets its own credentials and its own set of permissions.

## 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#what-is-a-notion-connection)

What is a Notion connection?

A Notion [connection](https://www.notion.so/help/add-and-manage-connections-with-the-api) — sometimes called an integration — connects your workspace to external apps and tools. That could be a SaaS product, an automation script, or a custom tool you’ve built. Connections are added to Notion workspaces and require **explicit permission** from users to access Notion pages and databases.

Create Notion connections that unlock new possibilities for teams.

Notion already has a [library](https://www.notion.so/integrations/all) of connections you can browse. For developers who want to build their own, Notion supports internal connections, public connections, and personal access tokens — all powered by the same REST API.

## 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#connection-types)

Connection types

Notion supports three authentication models:

- **Internal connections** are scoped to a single workspace and use a static API token. They’re ideal for custom automations and workflows — things like syncing data, sending notifications, or building internal dashboards.
- **Public connections** use OAuth 2.0 for authentication. At creation time, you choose their [installation scope](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/public-connections#installation-scope): **Any workspace** (any Notion user can install; Marketplace-eligible) or **Selected workspaces only** (restricted to workspaces you select; not Marketplace-eligible).
- **Personal access tokens (PATs)** are user-scoped tokens for scripts, CLI workflows, Workers, and tools that should act as one Notion user. A PAT uses the creator’s workspace membership and page permissions. See [Personal access tokens](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/personal-access-tokens).

Public connections must undergo a Notion security review before being [listed on the Marketplace](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/marketplace-listing). You can create and use a public connection without listing it.

### 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#comparison)

Comparison

| Feature | Internal connections | Public connections | Personal access tokens |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Best for | Team-owned automations in one workspace. | Apps or services used by many Notion users or workspaces. | User-owned scripts, CLI workflows, Workers, and trusted tools. |
| Installation scope | Single workspace. | Any workspace, or a specific set of workspaces chosen at creation time. Scope can’t change after creation. | One user in one workspace. |
| User access | Only members of the workspace where it’s installed. | Any user in a workspace where the connection is allowed to install. | The member who created the token. |
| Content access | Granted directly to the connection, not tied to any specific user. | Users choose which pages to share during the OAuth flow or via the Add connections menu. | Uses the creator’s Notion permissions; pages do not need to be shared with a bot. |
| Authentication | Static API token. | OAuth 2.0. | Static bearer token. |

**Looking for SCIM or SAML SSO?**Enterprise identity management (user provisioning, group management, and Single Sign-On) is covered in Notion’s Help Center, not in these API docs.

[**Provision users and groups with SCIM**](https://www.notion.so/help/provision-users-and-groups-with-scim)

[**SAML SSO configuration**](https://www.notion.so/help/saml-sso-configuration)

## 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#shared-concepts)

Shared concepts

All connection and token types share a few core concepts.

### 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#capabilities)

Capabilities

Every connection or token has a set of capabilities that control what it can do — read content, update content, insert content, read comments, and more. You configure capabilities when you create a connection or PAT. See the [Capabilities reference](https://developers.notion.com/reference/capabilities) for the full list.

[**Pages**\\ \\ Create, update, and retrieve page content.](https://developers.notion.com/guides/data-apis/working-with-page-content)

[**Databases**\\ \\ Manage database, properties, entries, and schemas.](https://developers.notion.com/guides/data-apis/working-with-databases)

[**Views**\\ \\ Create and configure database views programmatically.](https://developers.notion.com/guides/data-apis/working-with-views)

[**Data sources**\\ \\ Manage data sources, properties, entries, and schemas.](https://developers.notion.com/reference/data-source)

[**File uploads**\\ \\ Upload and attach files to pages and databases.](https://developers.notion.com/guides/data-apis/working-with-files-and-media)

[**Comments**\\ \\ Handle page and inline comments.](https://developers.notion.com/guides/data-apis/working-with-comments)

[**Content queries**\\ \\ Search through workspace content.](https://developers.notion.com/reference/post-search)

[**Users**\\ \\ Access user profiles and permissions.](https://developers.notion.com/reference/user)

### 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#content-access)

Content access

Connections must have access to pages and databases before they can interact with them. The mechanism differs by type:

- **Internal connections** can be granted access in two ways: the connection owner can add pages directly from the **Content access** tab in the Developer portal, or workspace members can share pages via the **Add connections** menu in Notion.
- **Public connections** use the OAuth page picker, where users select which pages to grant access to during the authorization flow.
- **Personal access tokens** use the token creator’s existing Notion permissions. If the creator can access a page in Notion, a PAT with the right capabilities can access it through the API.

See the [Internal connections](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/internal-connections), [Public connections](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/public-connections), and [Personal access tokens](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/personal-access-tokens) guides for specifics on how content access works for each type.

### 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#webhooks)

Webhooks

Connections can subscribe to real-time events — like page updates, property changes, and new comments — via webhooks. This allows your connection to react to changes in Notion without polling the API. See the [Webhooks guide](https://developers.notion.com/reference/webhooks) for details on setting up webhook subscriptions.

## 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#starting-your-connection-journey)

Starting your connection journey

We recommend starting with an internal connection — it’s the fastest way to begin building. You get an API token immediately and can focus entirely on using the API within your workspace, without worrying about OAuth or Marketplace listing. You can always create a public connection later if you need multi-workspace support. Here’s a guided path through the documentation:

1

[**Quickstart**](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/quick-start) — Build your first connection with a hands-on tutorial.

2

[**Personal access tokens**](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/personal-access-tokens) — Create a user-scoped token for scripts, CLI workflows, Workers, or trusted tools.

3

[**Internal connections**](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/internal-connections) — Understand how internal connections work, including the permissions model.

4

[**Public connections**](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/public-connections) — Learn how public connections work, including installation scope and the OAuth flow.

5

[**Authorization**](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/authorization) — Implement the OAuth 2.0 flow for public connections.

6

[**Handling API keys**](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/handling-api-keys) — Secure and manage your API tokens in production.

7

[**Preparing for users**](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/preparing-for-users) — Set up databases, pages, and views automatically when users install your connection.

8

[**List on the Marketplace**](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/marketplace-listing) — Make your public connection discoverable to all Notion users.

## 

[​](https://developers.notion.com/#resources)

Resources

Explore the links below to get started, and join the [Notion Devs Slack community](https://join.slack.com/t/notiondevs/shared_invite/zt-3u9oid9q8-HLUBmMVWYK~g9HFo4U4raA) to share your projects and connect with fellow developers.

[**API reference**](https://developers.notion.com/reference/intro)

[**Notion SDK for JavaScript**](https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-js)

[**Starter templates**](https://github.com/makenotion/notion-sdk-typescript-starter)

[**Postman collection**](https://www.postman.com/notionhq/notion-s-api-workspace/collection/52041987-03f70d8f-b6e5-4306-805c-f95f7cdf05b9)

[**FAQs**](https://developers.notion.com/page/frequently-asked-questions)

[**Notion Devs Slack**](https://join.slack.com/t/notiondevs/shared_invite/zt-3u9oid9q8-HLUBmMVWYK~g9HFo4U4raA)

[Developer quickstart\\ \\ Next](https://developers.notion.com/guides/get-started/quick-start)

Ctrl+I

[x](https://x.com/NotionDevs) [slack](https://join.slack.com/t/notiondevs/shared_invite/zt-3u9oid9q8-HLUBmMVWYK~g9HFo4U4raA)

Docs

# The next gen of notes & docs

Simple. Powerful. Beautiful. Communicate more efficiently with Notion's flexible building blocks.

[Get Notion free](https://notion.com/signup?from=marketing&pathname=%2Fproduct%2Fdocs&tid=ad03e07dac9a4b6d8c4dea9b8a815484) [Request a demo](https://notion.com/contact-sales)

Meeting Notes

Connect people & projects with updates & action items.

[Duplicate Template→](https://notion.com/templates/meeting-notes)

[Duplicate Template→](https://notion.com/templates/meeting-notes)

Design System

All your company's design assets in one place.

[Duplicate Template→](https://notion.com/templates/design-system)

[Duplicate Template→](https://notion.com/templates/design-system)

Project requirements

A customizable PRD that fits any type of project.

[Duplicate Template→](https://notion.com/templates/prd)

[Duplicate Template→](https://notion.com/templates/prd)

Pitch deck

Tell your company's story in a dynamic way.

[Duplicate Template→](https://notion.com/templates/pitch-deck)

[Duplicate Template→](https://notion.com/templates/pitch-deck)

AI meeting notes

AI meeting notes

AI meeting notes

AI meeting notes

## Go way beyond text & bullet points

### Code snippets

Native syntax highlighting for dozens of languages.

### Toggles

Collapsible sections make your docs easy to read.

### Images & videos

Embed directly from Loom & YouTube, or upload your own.

### Table of contents

Click to jump to a section. Updates automatically.

### Charts

Add live charts to any doc.

### And 50+ more content types

Like a bottomless box of building blocks.

## Store docs where people can easily find them

### Every team’s files, at a glance

Notion’s sidebar keeps your workspace organized no matter how fast you grow.

> Not only do our streamlined workflows in Notion save us time, they also make it easier to stay up to date on task details and progress.

Taku Wakasugi

Toyota Frontier Research Center, Toyota

## Get your team on the same page, literally

### Collaborate or co-edit together

Allow others to comment or suggest edits. Just type the @ key to get their attention.

### Comments keep the ball rolling async

A consolidated view of feedback makes it easy to iterate, even across time zones.

> We can quickly build out a meeting agenda for a large group of people and still make it interactive and fun.

**Scott Stephens**Design Operations Manager, Kin + Carta

## For PMs, designers, engineers, and everyone in between

Product managers

Connect the roadmap to goals, and keep folks aligned on what’s shipping and when.

[See how PMs use Notion→](https://notion.com/product/notion-for-product)

Designers

Move review rounds forward, prioritize requests, and hit all your creative deadlines.

[See how designers use Notion→](https://notion.com/product/notion-for-design)

Engineers

Ship features faster with sprints, code guidelines, bug fixes & more, all in one place.

[See how engineers use Notion→](https://notion.com/product/notion-for-engineering)

## Wikis and Projects included, for the same price

### Wikis

It's hard to move fast with a clunky & disorganized workspace. Centralize all your knowledge in Notion instead.

### Projects

Manage any type of project, no matter the team or size.

## Info from Figma, GitHub & more. Easy to share, easy to see.

[See all connections](https://notion.com/integrations/all)

### Figma

Always share the most updated designs with your team.

[Try now→](https://notion.com/integrations/figma)

### Amplitude

Track release metrics, experiment results, and more.

[Try now→](https://notion.com/integrations/amplitude)

### Github

Bring pull requests, issues, and even repos directly into Notion.

[Try now→](https://notion.com/integrations/github)

### Slack

Simply paste a link to see the full message without switching tools.

[Try now→](https://notion.com/integrations/slack)

### Jira

See bug updates, or convert an entire project into a database.

[Try now→](https://notion.com/integrations/jira)

### Browse all connections

[Go to the gallery→](https://notion.com/integrations/all)

## Choose a template to get started

[Explore templates](https://notion.com/templates)

### Mixpanel’s daily standup & tasks

[Try template→](https://notion.com/templates/mixpanels-daily-standup-and-tasks)

### Notion's product requirement doc

[Try template→](https://notion.com/templates/product-requirement-document-prd)

### Netflix's branding framework

[Try template→](https://notion.com/templates/netflix-branding-framework)

# Write your next doc in Notion

Play around with it first. Pay and add your team later.

[Get Notion free](https://notion.com/signup?from=marketing&pathname=%2Fproduct%2Fdocs&tid=ad03e07dac9a4b6d8c4dea9b8a815484) [Request a demo](https://notion.com/contact-sales)
